sound_type: haunting yowl (500-2,000 Hz) + prey-tracking chatter (1,000-4,000 Hz). E=5. FELIDAE QUARTET COMPLETE.
ecosystem: Boreal forest. Dense coniferous, deep snow, subalpine. Snow specialist.
season: Year-round resident. Yowl peaks in breeding season. Chatter year-round when hunting.
family: Felidae (fourth and final entry -- QUARTET COMPLETE)

coordinates.description: Canada Lynx in North Cascades and Okanogan region. High-elevation boreal forest.

taxonomy_note.description: FELIDAE QUARTET COMPLETE (212-215). Fourth and final Felidae entry. Three species, four degrees.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Felidae: Cougar Scream (212, E=7), Cougar Chirp (213, E=4), Bobcat Yowl (214, E=5), Canada Lynx (215, E=5). COMPLETE.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Lynx canadensis = 'Canadian lynx'. Lynx from Greek 'to shine' (bright eyes). canadensis for range.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Lynx -- 4 species. Bobcat (214) and Canada Lynx (215) both in PNW. Same genus, different niches.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Fourth Felidae entry. Quartet complete.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Felidae: final entry. Specialist closing the family after generalist (bobcat) and apex (cougar).
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. 8-14 kg. Snow specialist. Federally threatened. Fewer than 100 in WA.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Yowl: 500-2,000 Hz. Chatter: 1,000-4,000 Hz. Hiss/growl: broad.
song_metrics.note_structure: Yowl: haunting, ethereal, less raspy than bobcat. Chatter: rapid clicking.
song_metrics.note_types: Mating yowl (haunting, territorial), prey-tracking chatter (excited, hunting), hiss (defensive), growl (warning).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Yowl sequences during mating. Chatter triggered by prey sighting.
song_metrics.timbre: Yowl: haunting, ghostly, melancholic. Chatter: rapid, clicking, excited.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Moderate, focused, specialist. Same as bobcat.
song_metrics.tempo: Yowl: slow, drawn-out. Chatter: rapid, staccato.
song_metrics.context: Yowl: breeding season, territorial. Chatter: hunting, prey-tracking.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal variation. Snow-adapted populations throughout range.
song_metrics.key_property: CANADA LYNX. Yowl + chatter. E=5. FELIDAE QUARTET COMPLETE. Snow specialist. v1.49.356.

pelage.overall: Silver-gray to yellowish-brown. Long facial ruff. Prominent ear tufts. Short black-tipped tail.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Large ear tufts. Facial ruff. Short tail with black tip. Massive snowshoe paws.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Thicker, paler coat in winter. Darker, thinner in summer.

physical_description.overall: 8-14 kg (18-31 lbs). Body length 73-107 cm. Short tail 5-13 cm. Long legs. Massive paws.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Massive snowshoe paws. Prominent ear tufts. Facial ruff. Short black-tipped tail.
physical_description.field_id: Medium gray cat with massive paws, ear tufts, and facial ruff. Extremely rare in WA. If seen, report to WDFW. Do not approach.

nodes.title: FELIDAE QUARTET COMPLETE (212-215)
nodes.description: Three species, four degrees. Cougar scream (E=7), chirp (E=4), bobcat yowl (E=5), Canada Lynx (E=5). All PNW wild felids.

nodes.title: Snow Specialist -- Extreme Adaptation
nodes.description: Massive snowshoe paws. Deep-snow boreal forest. Fewer than 100 in WA. Federally threatened.

nodes.title: Lynx-Hare Cycle -- Classic Ecology
nodes.description: 10-year boom-bust cycle. 75-97% dietary dependence. Documented since 1700s. Predator fate tied to prey.

cross_references.relationship: SAME GENUS! Both Lynx. Generalist (20K+) vs. specialist (<100 in WA). Same E=5.
FELIDAE BOOKENDS! Cougar opens (E=7), lynx closes (E=5). Apex to specialist.
NEXT FAMILY! Felidae closes, Mustelidae returns. Snow to fierce.
PRECISION! Math rock (E=6) and snow-hunting (E=5). The math and the snow.
